title: Phaedo Fingerprint
description: A structured, layered model of how a subject thinks, communicates, decides, and works (Phaedo Protocol v0.1 §4). The decrypted vault payload; the ciphertext envelope (§7) is the only form transported. Tightened (2026-06-15) to the §4.1/§4.2 prose AND to what the reference producer actually emits (extraction/fingerprint-update.js + migration.js + the assembled fingerprint): the layer envelope and per-signal shape are typed and bounded, and the §4.1 protocol-identity fields (phaedo_protocol_version, subject_id, schema_revision) are now REQUIRED — the reference producer stamps them via migration.js stampProtocolFields. The producer↔schema tie is spec/test-schemas.mjs (validates the shipped sample AND live extraction output); signal uniqueness per (field, polarity, domain) — which JSON Schema cannot express across array items — is enforced there too.

phaedo_fingerprint.description: The §4.1 fingerprint document. vault-data.schema.json $refs this sub-schema as the runtime payload's fingerprint.

phaedo_protocol_version.description: Protocol version (MAJOR.MINOR), §4.1. Emitted by the reference producer (migration.js stampProtocolFields).

fingerprint_id.description: Stable identifier for this fingerprint instance (§4.1, UUID v4).

subject_id.description: Opaque, non-PII subject identifier (§4.1). A UUID minted once per fingerprint by the reference producer; never derived from PII.

updated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent layer update (§4.1).

persona_strength.description: Producer confidence the fingerprint is a useful match (§4.4).

schema_revision.description: Layer-schema revision counter (§4.1). Emitted by the reference producer (migration.js stampProtocolFields).

layers.description: The eight canonical layers: surface, behavioral, creative, domain_and_expertise, temporal_and_context, collaboration_and_relationship, decision_and_risk, big_picture. Each is independently selectable and encryptable. Any combination MAY be absent (§4.2).

consult_policies.description: DEPRECATED (Proposal 0007) — superseded by `standing_rules` (kind:authorization). Retained as a back-compat read path; existing documents stay valid and the resolver still honors them. See consultation-policy.schema.json.

standing_rules.description: Subject-AUTHORED standing instructions (§5.2 shape) — the single home for authored rules (Proposal 0007), carried on the fingerprint as the v0.1 A-home (supersedes the standalone consult_policies array; migrates to the §5 User Configuration sibling in v0.2). Authored content: stated once, no producer confidence score, applied VERBATIM, never inferred. The extraction pipeline MUST NOT write this array (locked by scripts/test_standing_rules_invariant.mjs). `kind:"instruction"` is a verbatim rule, optionally bound to a Decision&Risk dimension via `decision` (which marks that dimension authored-canonical in §4.7 resolution). `kind:"authorization"` is a deterministic §10.5 gate (match/effect, shared $defs); if it also carries `text` it injects too.

text.description: Verbatim rule (§5.2). UTF-8, NFC. Injected as-is — MUST NOT be summarized. Required for instruction; optional for authorization (present → the gate also injects).

scope.description: §5.2 scope. v0.1: only `global` normative; use `decision.domain` / `match.domains` for v0.1 domain scoping.

priority.description: §5.2 ordering hint; lower injects first. Ties broken by created_at.

decision.description: OPTIONAL (instruction). Binding to one Decision&Risk dimension; present → the projection/consultation treats it authored-canonical (§4.7 'authored overrides inferred').

polarity.description: §4.3. A negative authored rule is canonical over a contradicting inferred positive.

value.description: OPTIONAL catalog value token for the bound dimension (e.g. `quality`, `ask`, `more_cautious`). Carried when the rule came from a selected template (`source:"interview_template"`); lets a future resolver bind a precise value rather than just a cautious nudge. Free-text / edited rules omit it.

domain.description: OPTIONAL domain scope, same case-insensitive substring namespace as action_descriptor.domain / match.domains.

origin.description: OPTIONAL — the act-for-me vs act-as-me channel (mirrors signals[].origin). Absent/`self` = how the subject decides themselves. `delegation` = how they want an agent to act on their behalf — carried when the rule was endorsed from a delegation-promoted suggestion (`source:"delegation_promotion"`), so the resolver/projection treat it as the act-as-me preference for that dimension.

match.description: AUTHORIZATION only — §10.5 matcher. Optional (matchless = matches all).

effect.description: AUTHORIZATION only — §10.5 effect. Required when kind=authorization.

note.description: AUTHORIZATION only — resolver rationale_hint source (back-compat with consult_policies.note).

expires_at.description: AUTHORIZATION only — OPTIONAL RFC 3339. Conditional/revocable authority (§10.5): the authorization lapses (stops applying) once this instant passes. ENFORCED (authority-narrowing, fail-safe).

agents.description: AUTHORIZATION only — OPTIONAL, RESERVED. Scope to specific agent identities; NOT yet enforced (awaits authenticated agent identity — a self-asserted agent_id would be false security).

source.description: OPTIONAL provenance: how the rule was authored — hand-written in onboarding (`interview`), endorsed from a selected seed template in onboarding (`interview_template`), by endorsing a delta-promoted inferred-pattern suggestion (`delta_promotion`), or by endorsing a delegation-promoted suggestion built from the subject's consult override history (`delegation_promotion`, an act-as-me rule). All are authored: endorsement is the write.

suggested_rules.description: Delta-promotion STAGING queue (Proposal 0007 §E) — NOT authored content. When the extraction engine's inferred Decision&Risk signals show a pattern recurring (observed/corroborated, observation_count ≥ floor) that is not already an authored rule, the producer MAY stage a `suggested` candidate here for the subject to review. This is the one place the producer writes a rule-shaped object — and it is explicitly a *proposal*, never injected and never consulted. Only the subject's endorsement (rule-review.js `endorseSuggestion`) turns a suggestion into a `standing_rules` entry; that endorsement is the authored write. A rejected suggestion stays here with `status:"dismissed"` so it is not re-proposed.

status.description: `suggested` awaits the subject; `dismissed` was rejected (kept for audit + to suppress re-proposal).

source.description: How the suggestion was produced: `delta_promotion` = the extraction engine staged an established INFERRED Decision&Risk pattern (Proposal 0007 §E); `delegation_promotion` = the MCP server staged an established pattern from the subject's consult OVERRIDE history (an act-as-me suggestion — `decision.origin` is `delegation`).

proposed_text.description: A first-person candidate rule the review UI shows; the subject edits it before endorsing.

decision.description: The Decision&Risk dimension the recurring signal sits on (becomes the endorsed rule's binding).

origin.description: OPTIONAL — `delegation` for a delegation-promoted suggestion, so endorsing it authors an act-as-me standing rule (`decision.origin:"delegation"`).

evidence.description: The delta cluster the suggestion rests on — provenance, never injected.

conflict_records.description: §4.7 conflict STAGING queue — NOT authored content, NOT injected, NOT consulted. When Loop-2 reconciliation detects a new `observed` value contradicting a `self_reported` one below the §4.7 evidence floor (a contradiction the confidence merge would otherwise collapse), the producer MAY stage it here for the subject to review. Derived from the evidence log; carries both sides' provenance. Auto-closes (`resolved`) once the inferred side crosses the floor; a subject's `dismissed` is sticky. Reference: extraction/reconcile.js `detectConflicts`/`reconcileFingerprint`.

layer.description: Layer id the contradicted dimension sits on.

domain.description: OPTIONAL domain scope (per-domain signals are separate dimensions).

status.description: `open` awaits the subject; `resolved` = the inferred side later crossed the floor; `dismissed` = subject dismissed (sticky, not re-opened).

last_seen.description: RFC 3339 — most recent pass that re-observed the contradiction.

candidates.description: Both sides — the §4.7 provenance of the contradicting signals.

resolution.description: How the record was resolved. `by:'system'` = auto-resolution: sustained behavior crossed the §4.7 floor and overrode a self-report — surfaced for review (acknowledge/revert). `by:'user'` = subject acted (settle/acknowledge/revert).

chosen_value.description: The value the record resolved toward.

prior_value.description: On a system override, the self-reported value that was overridden — what revert restores to.

ack_at.description: RFC 3339 — when the subject acted on a system override (acknowledge or revert).

reverted_to.description: On revert, the value restored (== prior_value).

extensions.description: Producer-specific or experimental fields (§4.5). Keys SHOULD be producer-prefixed; consumers MUST ignore unknown keys.

phaedo_consult_policies.description: Optional portable consultation guardrails — see consultation-policy.schema.json.

layer.description: A §4.2 layer document. Shape varies by provenance: questionnaire-seeded layers carry label/responses/summary/signals; extraction-built layers carry layer_version/last_updated/confidence/signals/summary. All properties typed; none individually required so both forms validate.

layer_version.description: Layer schema version, independent of the top-level protocol version (§4.2).

last_updated.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent observation that updated this layer (§4.2).

confidence.description: Producer confidence in this layer specifically (§4.2).

summary.description: Derived natural-language summary for injection (Loop-2 extraction; v0.1 limit 2000 chars).

signal.description: A structured §4.2/§5.1 signal. field + polarity + value are the load-bearing trio every signal carries. The Decision & Risk layer additionally has a normative per-signal schema (decision-risk-signal.schema.json).

dimension.description: Proposal 0002 alias of field (the decision/style axis).

value.description: Enum token, scalar, array, or number — resolved by the producer; consumers tolerate any JSON value.

source.description: e.g. interview, questionnaire, extraction, delta, observation.

evidence_basis.description: How the signal was learned (proposal 0002); consumers weigh observed/corroborated above self_reported.

origin.description: OPTIONAL — the act-for-me vs act-as-me channel. Absent/`self` = learned from the subject's OWN behavior. `delegation` = learned from the subject CORRECTING an agent acting on their behalf — a distinct preference class (how they want work done FOR them, which need not match how they do it themselves). CONSUMED by the reference resolver as of v0.1.29: in a consultation (an agent about to act on the subject's behalf) a `delegation` signal OUTRANKS a `self` one for the same dimension. Producer EMISSION of `delegation`-origin signals (the capture/outcome source) is the next step; until then all signals are `self`.

domain.description: Optional §4.2.7 domain scope (substring namespace shared with action_descriptor.domain).

last_observed.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent observation.
